title: thymus maroccanus subsp. maroccanus
intro: Thymus maroccanus subsp. maroccanus, also known as Moroccan thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant that is native to Morocco and is characterized by its small, fragrant leaves and pink or purple flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, primarily for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and sore throats. The medicinal properties of Moroccan thyme also provide health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Moroccan thyme can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be applied topically to the skin to treat wounds and infections.
